meta.title: Cleavage of T4-induced proteins during phage morphogenesis: characterization of peptides -- Benchetrit and Bachrach 31 (2): 163 -- Journal of General Virology

abstractHtml: Polypeptides of low mol. wt. have been extracted from T4 coliphages and from Escherichia coli B cells infected with a wild type and various amber mutants of bacteriophage T4. Six peptides were fractionated by chromatography on phosphocellulose: three of them were cleaved from proteins synthesized late in infection and related to phage head. The remaining three peptides have been shown to arise from early-labelled phage-induced proteins. Two of these six small petide fragments were found in the head of the T4 phage.
